title: Verification Results Recording and Listing

story.i_want_to: record structured verification results when I verify or reject a story and list the full verification history
story.so_that: there is a detailed, immutable audit trail of every verification attempt including findings, review types, and pass/fail/partial outcomes

rationale: Verification results are the permanent record of the orchestrator's independent assessment. Unlike the verified_status field on a story (which shows only the current state), verification_results capture every assessment over time. This history is critical for understanding why a story was rejected, what was found during review, and how many verification cycles a story went through before passing. The findings jsonb field allows rich structured data (missing files, failing tests, code quality issues) that goes far beyond a simple pass/fail.

acceptance_criteria.description: POST /api/v1/stories/:id/verify (from US-7.2) creates a verification_result record with result='pass', the provided summary, findings (jsonb), and review_type. The orchestrator_agent_id is resolved from the API key.
POST /api/v1/stories/:id/reject (from US-7.2) creates a verification_result record with result='fail', the rejection reason as summary, findings (jsonb), and review_type.

Orchestrators can also POST /api/v1/stories/:id/verify with result='partial' for stories that partially meet criteria. This sets verified_status to 'verified' but the verification_result records result='partial' for nuanced tracking.
GET /api/v1/stories/:id/verifications returns a list of all verification_result records for the story, ordered by inserted_at descending (most recent first). Available to orchestrator and user roles.
Each verification_result record includes: id, tenant_id, story_id, orchestrator_agent_id, result (pass/fail/partial), summary (text), findings (jsonb), review_type (string), and inserted_at.
Verification results are immutable — no update or delete endpoints exist. Each verify or reject creates a new record.
GET /api/v1/stories/:id/verifications returns 403 for agent-role API keys. Agents cannot see verification details (only the verified_status on the story).
All endpoints enforce tenant isolation via RLS. Cross-tenant requests return 404.
The findings field accepts arbitrary JSON structures. Common patterns include {missing_artifacts: [...], failing_tests: [...], code_issues: [...]} but any valid JSON is accepted.
The review_type field is free-form string. Common values include 'enhanced_review', 'artifact_check', 'manual', 'automated', but any string is accepted.

test_cases.name: Verify creates verification_result with pass
steps: POST /api/v1/stories/:id/verify with body {summary: 'All 8 acceptance criteria met', findings: {criteria_checked: 8, criteria_passed: 8, artifacts_found: ['migration', 'schema', 'test']}, review_type: 'enhanced_review'}
expected_results: GET verifications returns list with 1 record
Record has result='pass', correct summary, findings jsonb with criteria data, review_type='enhanced_review', orchestrator_agent_id matching the orch agent

test_cases.name: Reject creates verification_result with fail
steps: POST /api/v1/stories/:id/reject with body {reason: 'No migration file found', findings: {missing_artifacts: ['migration'], checked_paths: ['priv/repo/migrations/']}, review_type: 'artifact_check'}
expected_results: verification_result created with result='fail', summary matching reason, correct findings and review_type

test_cases.name: Verification history accumulates across multiple verify/reject cycles
steps: Manually reset story verified_status to unverified for re-check
POST /api/v1/stories/:id/reject with reason 'Tests present but 2 failing'
Manually reset story verified_status to unverified for re-check
expected_results: Records ordered by inserted_at descending: pass, fail, fail
Each record has distinct summary and inserted_at
Full history preserved despite story's verified_status only reflecting latest state

test_cases.name: Agent-role key cannot list verification results
Tenant isolation: cross-tenant verification listing returns 404
steps: GET /api/v1/stories/:story_a_id/verifications with tenant B orchestrator key
expected_results: No verification results from tenant A are exposed

test_cases.name: Partial verification result
steps: POST /api/v1/stories/:id/verify with body {summary: '6 of 8 criteria met, deferring 2 non-critical', result: 'partial', findings: {criteria_passed: 6, criteria_deferred: 2}, review_type: 'enhanced_review'}
expected_results: Findings include the partial assessment details

test_cases.name: Rich findings jsonb is stored and retrieved correctly
steps: Create verification_result with deeply nested findings: {categories: [{name: 'tests', items: [{file: 'test.exs', status: 'pass'}, {file: 'test2.exs', status: 'fail', error: 'assertion failed'}]}, {name: 'artifacts', items: [{type: 'migration', exists: true}]}]}
Retrieve the verification_result and inspect findings
expected_results: Findings jsonb is stored and retrieved with full fidelity
Nested structures, arrays, and mixed types are preserved

technical_notes: Implement Loopctl.Verifications context module with create_verification_result/2 and list_verification_results/2.

verification_results table: id (uuid PK), tenant_id (uuid FK), story_id (uuid FK), orchestrator_agent_id (uuid FK), result (enum: pass/fail/partial), summary (text), findings (jsonb default {}), review_type (string), inserted_at (utc_datetime).
The creation of verification_result records is already done within the verify/reject Ecto.Multi in US-7.2. This story adds the listing endpoint and ensures the schema/context module is complete.
Controller at LoopctlWeb.VerificationController with index action, nested under /api/v1/stories/:story_id/verifications.
No update or delete — this is an append-only table for audit integrity.
Agent-role keys are explicitly denied access to verification results to maintain the trust boundary.
Schema module MUST use `use Loopctl.Schema` (provides `@primary_key {:id, :binary_id, autogenerate: true}`, `@foreign_key_type :binary_id`, `@timestamps_opts [type: :utc_datetime_usec]`)
All context functions MUST return `{:ok, result}` or `{:error, reason}` tuples. Never raise exceptions for expected error cases.
Controller MUST use `action_fallback LoopctlWeb.FallbackController` for error rendering (maps error atoms to HTTP status codes per US-1.4)

Testing infrastructure: (1) All tests use async: true via DataCase/ConnCase, (2) Mox mocks defined in test/support/mocks.ex, (3) Default permissive stubs set in DataCase/ConnCase setup via stub_all_defaults/0 and Mox.set_mox_from_context(tags) for async isolation, (4) setup :verify_on_exit! in every test module, (5) Fixtures in test/support/fixtures.ex with build/2 (no DB) and fixture/2 (DB insert with auto-dependency resolution), (6) NEVER Application.put_env in tests — all service swapping via config/test.exs.
Config-based DI: behaviours resolved via Application.get_env(:loopctl, :key, DefaultModule). config/test.exs maps behaviours to Mox mocks. Tests use expect/3 to set specific expectations.
